## AI Generation Attributes

Attributes for AI content generation, combining OTel GenAI and custom

| Attribute | Type | Description | Examples | Stability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <a id="commit-story-ai-section-type" href="#commit-story-ai-section-type">`commit_story.ai.section_type`</a> | string | The type of journal section being generated | `summary`; `dialogue`; `technical_decisions` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-operation-name" href="#gen-ai-operation-name">`gen_ai.operation.name`</a> | string | The name of the operation being performed. [1] | `chat`; `generate_content`; `text_completion` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-provider-name" href="#gen-ai-provider-name">`gen_ai.provider.name`</a> | string | The Generative AI provider as identified by the client or server instrumentation. [2] | `openai`; `gcp.gen_ai`; `gcp.vertex_ai` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-request-max-tokens" href="#gen-ai-request-max-tokens">`gen_ai.request.max_tokens`</a> | int | The maximum number of tokens the model generates for a request. | `100` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-request-model" href="#gen-ai-request-model">`gen_ai.request.model`</a> | string | The name of the GenAI model a request is being made to. [3] | `gpt-4` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-request-temperature" href="#gen-ai-request-temperature">`gen_ai.request.temperature`</a> | double | The temperature setting for the GenAI request. | `0.0` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-response-id" href="#gen-ai-response-id">`gen_ai.response.id`</a> | string | The unique identifier for the completion. | `chatcmpl-123` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-response-model" href="#gen-ai-response-model">`gen_ai.response.model`</a> | string | The name of the model that generated the response. [4] | `gpt-4-0613` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-usage-input-tokens" href="#gen-ai-usage-input-tokens">`gen_ai.usage.input_tokens`</a> | int | The number of tokens used in the GenAI input (prompt). | `100` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="gen-ai-usage-output-tokens" href="#gen-ai-usage-output-tokens">`gen_ai.usage.output_tokens`</a> | int | The number of tokens used in the GenAI response (completion). | `180` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

**[1] `gen_ai.operation.name`:** If one of the predefined values applies, but specific system uses a different name it's RECOMMENDED to document it in the semantic conventions for specific GenAI system and use system-specific name in the instrumentation. If a different name is not documented, instrumentation libraries SHOULD use applicable predefined value.

**[2] `gen_ai.provider.name`:** The attribute SHOULD be set based on the instrumentation's best
knowledge and may differ from the actual model provider.

Multiple providers, including Azure OpenAI, Gemini, and AI hosting platforms
are accessible using the OpenAI REST API and corresponding client libraries,
but may proxy or host models from different providers.

The `gen_ai.request.model`, `gen_ai.response.model`, and `server.address`
attributes may help identify the actual system in use.

The `gen_ai.provider.name` attribute acts as a discriminator that
identifies the GenAI telemetry format flavor specific to that provider
within GenAI semantic conventions.
It SHOULD be set consistently with provider-specific attributes and signals.
For example, GenAI spans, metrics, and events related to AWS Bedrock
should have the `gen_ai.provider.name` set to `aws.bedrock` and include
applicable `aws.bedrock.*` attributes and are not expected to include
`openai.*` attributes.

**[3] `gen_ai.request.model`:** The name of the GenAI model a request is being made to. If the model is supplied by a vendor, then the value must be the exact name of the model requested. If the model is a fine-tuned custom model, the value should have a more specific name than the base model that's been fine-tuned.

**[4] `gen_ai.response.model`:** If available. The name of the GenAI model that provided the response. If the model is supplied by a vendor, then the value must be the exact name of the model actually used. If the model is a fine-tuned custom model, the value should have a more specific name than the base model that's been fine-tuned.

---

`commit_story.ai.section_type` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value  | Description | Stability |
|---|---|---|
| `context_synthesis` | Multi-source context synthesis | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `dialogue` | Developer dialogue extraction | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `summary` | Daily summary section generation | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `technical_decisions` | Technical decisions analysis | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

---

`gen_ai.operation.name` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value  | Description | Stability |
|---|---|---|
| `chat` | Chat completion operation such as [OpenAI Chat API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `create_agent` | Create GenAI agent | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `embeddings` | Embeddings operation such as [OpenAI Create embeddings API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `execute_tool` | Execute a tool | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `generate_content` | Multimodal content generation operation such as [Gemini Generate Content](https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `invoke_agent` | Invoke GenAI agent | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `text_completion` | Text completions operation such as [OpenAI Completions API (Legacy)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

---

**[5]:** Used when accessing the 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com' endpoint. Also known as the AI Studio API.

**[6]:** May be used when specific backend is unknown.

**[7]:** Used when accessing the 'aiplatform.googleapis.com' endpoint.

## Commit Attributes

Attributes describing git commit data collected by commit-story

| Attribute | Type | Description | Examples | Stability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <a id="commit-story-commit-author" href="#commit-story-commit-author">`commit_story.commit.author`</a> | string | The commit author name | `Whitney Lee` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-commit-files-changed" href="#commit-story-commit-files-changed">`commit_story.commit.files_changed`</a> | int | Number of files changed in the commit | `3`; `12` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-commit-message" href="#commit-story-commit-message">`commit_story.commit.message`</a> | string | The first line of the commit message | `feat: add user authentication`; `fix: resolve memory leak in collector` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-commit-timestamp" href="#commit-story-commit-timestamp">`commit_story.commit.timestamp`</a> | string | The commit timestamp in ISO 8601 format | `2026-02-03T10:30:00Z` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="vcs-ref-head-name" href="#vcs-ref-head-name">`vcs.ref.head.name`</a> | string | The git branch name [8] | `my-feature-branch`; `tag-1-test` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="vcs-ref-head-revision" href="#vcs-ref-head-revision">`vcs.ref.head.revision`</a> | string | The full commit SHA hash [9] | `9d59409acf479dfa0df1aa568182e43e43df8bbe28d60fcf2bc52e30068802cc`; `main`; `123`; `HEAD` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

**[8] `vcs.ref.head.name`:** `head` refers to where you are right now; the current reference at a
given time.

**[9] `vcs.ref.head.revision`:** `head` refers to where you are right now; the current reference at a
given time.The revision can be a full [hash value (see
glossary)](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.186-5.pdf),
of the recorded change to a ref within a repository pointing to a
commit [commit](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-commit) object. It does
not necessarily have to be a hash; it can simply define a [revision
number](https://svnbook.red-bean.com/en/1.7/svn.tour.revs.specifiers.html)
which is an integer that is monotonically increasing. In cases where
it is identical to the `ref.head.name`, it SHOULD still be included.
It is up to the implementer to decide which value to set as the
revision based on the VCS system and situational context.

## Context Collection Attributes

Attributes for Claude Code session and context collection

| Attribute | Type | Description | Examples | Stability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <a id="commit-story-context-messages-count" href="#commit-story-context-messages-count">`commit_story.context.messages_count`</a> | int | Total number of messages collected from sessions | `42`; `156` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-context-sessions-count" href="#commit-story-context-sessions-count">`commit_story.context.sessions_count`</a> | int | Number of Claude Code sessions found and processed | `1`; `3` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-context-source" href="#commit-story-context-source">`commit_story.context.source`</a> | string | The source type of collected context | `claude_code`; `git`; `mcp` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-context-time-window-end" href="#commit-story-context-time-window-end">`commit_story.context.time_window_end`</a> | string | End of the collection time window (ISO 8601) | `2026-02-03T12:00:00Z` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-context-time-window-start" href="#commit-story-context-time-window-start">`commit_story.context.time_window_start`</a> | string | Start of the collection time window (ISO 8601) | `2026-02-03T08:00:00Z` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

`commit_story.context.source` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value  | Description | Stability |
|---|---|---|
| `claude_code` | Claude Code chat history from ~/.claude/projects/ | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `git` | Git commit and diff data | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `mcp` | MCP tool context capture | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

## Filter Attributes

Attributes for context filtering operations

|---|---|---|---|---|
| <a id="commit-story-filter-messages-after" href="#commit-story-filter-messages-after">`commit_story.filter.messages_after`</a> | int | Message count after filtering | `42` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-filter-messages-before" href="#commit-story-filter-messages-before">`commit_story.filter.messages_before`</a> | int | Message count before filtering | `156` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-filter-tokens-after" href="#commit-story-filter-tokens-after">`commit_story.filter.tokens_after`</a> | int | Token count after filtering | `15000` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-filter-tokens-before" href="#commit-story-filter-tokens-before">`commit_story.filter.tokens_before`</a> | int | Token count before filtering | `50000` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-filter-type" href="#commit-story-filter-type">`commit_story.filter.type`</a> | string | The type of filtering operation applied | `token_budget`; `noise_removal`; `sensitive_data` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

`commit_story.filter.type` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value  | Description | Stability |
|---|---|---|
| `noise_removal` | Removing tool calls and system messages | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `relevance` | Filtering by relevance to commit | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `sensitive_data` | Redacting sensitive information | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `token_budget` | Filtering based on token limits | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

## Journal Attributes

Attributes for journal entry generation and output

| Attribute | Type | Description | Examples | Stability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <a id="commit-story-journal-entry-date" href="#commit-story-journal-entry-date">`commit_story.journal.entry_date`</a> | string | The date of the journal entry (YYYY-MM-DD) | `2026-02-03` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-journal-file-path" href="#commit-story-journal-file-path">`commit_story.journal.file_path`</a> | string | Output file path for the journal entry | `journal/entries/2026-02/2026-02-03.md` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-journal-quotes-count" href="#commit-story-journal-quotes-count">`commit_story.journal.quotes_count`</a> | int | Number of developer quotes extracted for the entry | `5`; `12` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-journal-sections" href="#commit-story-journal-sections">`commit_story.journal.sections`</a> | string[] | List of sections generated in the journal entry | `["summary", "dialogue", "technical_decisions"]` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| <a id="commit-story-journal-word-count" href="#commit-story-journal-word-count">`commit_story.journal.word_count`</a> | int | Total word count of the generated journal entry | `450`; `1200` | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
